title: "You'll need journalism so distinctive it has its own gravity": New York Times publisher A.G. Sulzberger on how news organizations can stand up to AI companies
tldr: NYT publisher argues news orgs must produce distinctive journalism to survive AI's threat to the public square, calling out the profession's passivity. A strategic manifesto for the AI era of media.
why_fresh: high-profile strategic argument on AI vs journalism
angle: What 'distinctive enough to have its own gravity' means for brand content competing with AI summaries.

title: GEO six months on: the science is moving in favour of public relations
tldr: Waddington revisits generative engine optimization, arguing the risks are clearer and the opportunity bigger than first thought. A grounded take on how PR adapts to AI-driven discovery.
angle: GEO as the new SEO: how comms teams should restructure content for AI answer engines.

title: Social media is now three distinct categories
tldr: Researchers argue platforms have split into broadcasters, micro-communities, and AI-driven active experiences. A reframing of social strategy beyond the old feed model.
angle: Rebuilding a social media strategy around the broadcaster/micro-community/AI split.

title: The #1 Role Your Content Team Needs in 2026 Is a Managing Editor
tldr: As AI makes content cheap, editorial judgment becomes the differentiator — making the managing editor the key hire. A clear argument about where human value sits now.
why_fresh: role reframing in AI content era
angle: Why editorial judgment is the scarcest resource when everyone can generate infinite content.

title: When companies decide to say nothing
tldr: Drawing on USC Annenberg's 2026 Global Communication Report, Waddington analyzes when organizations choose to speak out on issues and when silence wins. Data-rich guide to issue management.
angle: The strategic case for staying silent: when brand purpose statements backfire.

title: YouTube's AI remix push exposes a looming reckoning for the creator economy
tldr: YouTube's Gemini Omni integration surfaces the deep problems generative AI poses for creators' rights and originality. A flashpoint for the creator economy's future.
why_fresh: new platform AI feature reshaping creators
angle: What AI remix means for content ownership and the value of original creator work.

title: Coca-Cola's AI-powered José Mourinho campaign could signal a shift in celebrity partnerships
tldr: Coke fronted a series with Mourinho's AI clone instead of the man himself, hinting at a new model for celebrity and influencer deals. A concrete sign of synthetic media going mainstream in brand work.
why_fresh: synthetic celebrity clone in major campaign
angle: AI clones of celebrities: the next frontier (and minefield) of brand storytelling.

title: How to prepare for more declines in traditional Google search traffic
tldr: With AI Overviews reducing clicks, experts share tactics for maintaining visibility as AI supplies the answers. Practical survival guide for content discovery.
angle: Building a content strategy for zero-click search and AI answer engines.

title: The emotional impact of working in communications
tldr: Waddington's research uncovers the rarely-named emotional weight senior comms work carries. A human angle on the toll of crisis and reputation work.
angle: The invisible emotional labor of being a senior communicator in permanent crisis mode.

title: The Future of Content Belongs to the Tastemakers
tldr: With AI making polished copy easy, taste and curation become the real differentiator that makes content memorable. A sharp argument on the new value of editorial point of view.
angle: Why taste, not output, is the content strategist's new competitive moat.

title: Layoffs don't have to feel inhumane
tldr: Argues layoffs are a trust moment, not just a messaging problem, and that employees judge leadership instantly in 2026. A reframe for internal comms and change management.
angle: Layoffs as the ultimate test of internal communications and leadership trust.

title: A viral space moment fell in Nutella's lap. Here's how they capitalized.
tldr: A floating Nutella jar on an Artemis II broadcast became a brand moment; speed and restraint made it work. A clean case study in real-time reactive marketing.
angle: The discipline of reactive marketing: when to pounce and when to step back.

title: Манифест для креаторов
tldr: Report from the Shorty Awards on the end of big brands', budgets' and glossy production's monopoly over creative work. A signal of power shifting to independent creators.
angle: The death of the glossy production monopoly and what it means for brand content.

title: AI Is Quietly Destroying Your Best Content
tldr: Argues that AI-driven repurposing is diluting brands' best content, with a rule and a quick test to check if you're already losing. Practical caution on automation in content ops.
angle: When content repurposing via AI erodes quality: governance rules to protect your best work.

title: Понимание трёх категорий онлайн-разговоров / Understanding the 3 categories of online chatter
tldr: Today's news emerges bottom-up from comment sections and social posts rather than top-down from gatekeepers; a single rumor can snowball. A useful framework for media monitoring and issue management.
angle: Mapping online chatter to catch issues before they become crises.
